reviews.review_msg: Wanted to share after seeing lots of negative reviews; I took this course in 2024 - easiest course at sfu. There's a 15 minute video lecture every week and assignments that help solidify understanding. Highly recommend if you did well in math/ physics it will be easy but most people that take it are in the health sci/ kin stream.

tip is to spend time reading the textbook. video not help much. Written midterm, pretty much the core basic of the whole course. The final was multiple choices, understand all the concepts, kind of basic math but you need to know when to use which formula. SPSS only need simple steps,try to have as much in4 in the graph as possible.TA not useful

Created an account on rate my prof just for this.
We have our final tomorrow and we haven't even had the opportunity to view our midterms (average: 15/40). No sample finals are given, and he sets very difficult exams.
The book (he wrote) is pretty much useless but all assignments are from it.
Don't repeat my mistake and save yourself.

Took this class online - it was a struggle. Short, unhelpful lecture videos and not much support from the professor or TAs. Unstuckstudy and the textbook were lifesavers for studying, but the exams were brutal. Not recommended.

I have a midterm with this guy tomorrow. He never told us what is on the exam (I just have to guess which chapters it is on), a 6 minute video per chapter, which is a glorified fit check with different jerseys and doesn't even have the courtesy of even giving us a practice test for what our midterm questions are going to be like. AVOID AT ALL COSTS

No lecture materials aside from very short (under 6 mins) videos, self-study from the required textbook.  Assignments are from the book but no feedback given.  Got a 0 on a question?  Go read the book again...
his recorded lectures were terrible. The squeaking from his dry erase marker made it 10x worse to watch. It came to a point where i just relied on the textbook only, goodluck

Paying tuition and self-learn the whole course. No slides for the course and the lecture video barely talks about the content from the textbook. Self-study the whole textbook and make sure you do well in assignments as they count for 40%. The final Exam with 60% is stressing people out and no one finished the final exam within the time limit
You have to be able to self-study to do well in this course. His videos tell you what to focus on in the textbook.

He really doesn't deserve the hate. STAT 270 online was incredibly straight forward: Read the text, watch the videos, do the practice questions. Assignments are short and simple, midterm was literally just questions from the textbook, and final although incredibly hard, is super curved. Got a 40% on it and still got an A-.

Horrific professor, took online but not accessible at all + TA is useless (no email provided to contact). Lectures are 6 mins and 1/2 of the time was him talking about his outfit?? Wrote the textbook so believes it will be enough, but extremely difficult to understand w/o support. Exams are difficult + lots of assignments. Don't waste your time.

Professor provided very little resources and help apart from posting short video clips skimming over textbook material. TAs were slow to respond and the entire course was essentially self-taught. There is a penalty scheme for the multiple choice final exam. I would recommend taking this course with another professor and in-person.

I'm pretty sure this guy just records his videos, posts them for his online class, and then just hides somewhere and let's the TA do the heavy lifting. Don't ever take this class. Only saving grace is the P/C/NC means I won't tank my GPA, though I'll never get my money back and I certainly won't take a class with him ever again.

Terrible professor, don't take this course with him. He will not help you, did not even come to midterms, and we couldn't even clarify questions with anyone because of the TA strike, and random volunteers were helping out with the midterm with no professor. He expects you to learn alone with no external help.
Worst prof ever. He has terrible lecture videos, doesn't even go over anything, and our exams are nonsense. He doesn't even have office hours. Don't take the class with him.

No Powerpoints, practice exercises or resources. Textbook contains very little Rstudio codes. In the syllabus, he says you will have to teach yourself codes through "googling." TA is very slow in answering emails and providing feedback. Good luck!

I've noticed the course materials and readings provided are somewhat limited. Having more diverse and comprehensive sources would greatly enrich our understanding of the subject matter. I felt certain concepts were not adequately explained in lectures. Providing more detailed explanation would greatly help. This course is frustrating.

In his videos, he skips important topics despite them being very significant for the course. He doesn't read the PowerPoint before presenting. Teaching yourself.TA is on strike yet he is doing. Your main support for questions is not there, he sent an email to everyone saying do not contact him unless you are sick or in dire need.

It is unconscionable and unfair to students that this online class, which is entirely reliant on TA's for any engagement or teaching, is going ahead with a midterm in spite of the TA strike. Brief prerecorded videos are of little to no help in teaching how to solve problems which make up the body of the course content.

Your main source of communication are your TAs, but with them going on strike it became very hard to get help. Provides pre-recorded lectures but they are less than 10 minutes and aren't that helpful. You would have to teach yourself most of the concepts if you are taking the course online. Avoid taking online course if you can

Worst professor I have ever had. Online provides 6 min videos per chapter, no practice exams, or power points, or examples. Doesn't teach anything, had to teach myself and get a tutor. Exams are extremely difficult and contain material that was never taught. Has penalty scheme that deducts points for wrong answers, making it impossible to pass.

Just know that for his online class, the lecture videos are of no help and the exams are completely different from the practice questions in the textbook. You are basically teaching yourself so don't expect any help from the prof or TAs bc they do not care! On top of that, he barely gives time for the exams so many students did not even finish it.

Worst professor ever, please avoid at all costs if possible. TAs don't care for your learning, you basically have to teach the whole course to yourself, and the textbook is useless. Exams are weighted so heavily, and he adds a penalty scheme on top of it all (deduction for the incorrect answer). One of the toughest profs ever.
forced against my will to buy, read, and "learn" from his textbook. worst textbook in the world, poorly written and doesn't explain anything well. somehow i know less about stats after reading it.
Useless confusing textbook that is mandatory but makes absolutely no sense

Tim Is an intelligent man, but he clearly lacks the ability to convey that knowledge onto his students in any meaningful manner. Literally I have nothing else to say. I attended every lecture and did all the assignments, asked for all the help I could get and I still got a bad mark. There was no avenue he provided to help me improve.

Dr. Swartz is by far the worst professor I've ever had. He seems bored with the course contents, spends large portions of lectures complaining about his own slides, and does not give any detailed examples. Students are expected to figure out how to actually solve problems by reading the textbook, which is also not helpful. Avoid if possible.

The exam of this class had nothing to do with what was in the textbook and was designed to fail you. I have a 4.0+ GPA, self-taught myself the entire textbook, got good marks on assignments, and only got a B.

If you are looking to maintain a high GPA or improve your GPA, NEVER take a class with Tim. Even if you put the work in you'll get a mediocre grade since the final exam has nothing to do with the rest of the course and is completely luck-based. It honestly amazes me that this man is allowed to teach.

Tim is the worst prof I've had so far. The final exam was worth 60% and covered material that was never taught. This prof is unbelievably lazy; he wasted our time with telling silly stories during lecture, and even said he wouldn't be responding to any emails for the semester. Any class with this prof is a GPA destroyer.

Very disengaged with the class. Most learning done through assigned textbook readings. Assignments and especially the exam were disconnected from the material covered. Some of the questions on the exam made me doubt he knew what content he was assigning to be covered. Final felt very luck based with 20 mc questions being worth 60% of the grade.

Textbook bad prof. Time to retire Tim. He's a 15+ year, and its getting to his head. Felt the need to use lecture time to ensure we were calling him Dr. Lectures aren't in any which way engaging, interesting, or beneficial. YouTube taught me this course. Wont talk to students, makes no effort to explain material. Classic line: "Use the TA's"

This class was awful! His online lectures are unhelpful so you're left to teach yourself with the textbook. His multiple-choice tests are a-k, they're wordy and confusing, and you don't get them back so you cant see where you went wrong. He doesn't offer relevant practice and is satisfied with a 40% class average. Worst teacher ever!

Like others have said, the exams are designed to trick you and are not representative of your knowledge (who gives a multiple choice question with 17 possible answers?). Both live and recorded lectures are unhelpful so you will have to teach most of the material to yourself. Just do yourself a favour and avoid if possible.

The hardest class I've ever taking thus far. He has optional lecture videos that are confusing and barely relate to the content. He provides weekly review worksheets however they are easy compared to what's on the exam. He does not prepare you for his exams at all and they are quite theory based so even the textbook can be little help.
not recommend this prof. He is cool himself but teach bad and his exams are not fair. even by studying hard you can't get a desirable mark.

First things first: The final exam is absolutely ridiculous. Even after spending days upon days studying the course material and doing the sample finals that he gave, the final was so detached from the rest of the course and was based off of luck, not knowledge. Save your money and GPA, do not take a course with Tim. Worst experience of my life.

Swartz does not care at all. He will not answer questions during class and if he does his answer is so convoluted it just makes students more confused. Exams are designed to trick students, not test understanding of materials and assignments are dull and overly long. 0/10 worst experience of my life

Tim radiates apathy. While I went into the course fully expecting to do a lot of independent study, I didn't expect his lectures to be so uninformative, low-effort, and pointless. The quality of teaching in this course was an absolute joke and I would not recommend taking it with Tim unless you are fully prepared to teach yourself.

STAY CLEAR! The exams are made for failure, not like practice or textbook questions. You can't go back after you finish a question and most of your time is wasted trying to read his poorly written questions. This course is more difficult than it needs to be, if only Tim would actually teach the material and not just read useless pre-made slides

Tim has got to be one of the worst professors at SFU. His lectures aren't good, basically a self-taught course. His practice questions are nothing like his exams. HIS EXAMS ARE INSANELY HARD, despite easy material, he purposely sets up students to fail. You cannot even ask him questions during his exam. AVOID AT ALL COSTS SAVE YOUR SANITY AND MONEY

Tim is the worst professor at SFU. He's so out of touch with reality that I'm unsure how he still has a job. Everything in this course is self taught. Exams are way different than practice and assignment questions. Course averages are low and test grades are based on luck rather than knowledge of material. Do not take this course with him!!!

Would not recommend taking this class, unless you want to teach yourself everything. The lectures are of no use and you'll probably retain the same information if you just go through the slides yourself. Majority of your grade is made up of the exams (80%). Also, the midterms are nothing like the questions in the assignments.

The course already has slides so he just reads off them and posts a zoom recording for lectures. He isnt bad at explaining new concepts, but his tests are almost comically difficult. He curves the test averages to be at 8 or 9/15, and gives multiple choice questions with answers A-P Hes clearly been teaching for too long and is so out of touch

Tim is a horrible professor. His lectures are uninformative and unengaging. He has absolutely no passion for the material. Exams are designed to trick students, rather than testing them on their knowledge of the material. I should've listened to the previous reviews. He was so bad!!! Do yourself a favour and avoid this professor.

Avoid Tim if you aren't naturally good at stats. Lectures are vague and not engaging. Midterms are very different from assignments and did not reflect competency. Textbook is an alright resource but not all answers to questions are provided, definitely buy the online version. Youtube will be your best friend and avoid online stats at all costs.
Testing was all multiple-choice. Ample opportunity to know the material perfectly but slip up on poorly worded or vague questions.

His lectures are almost useless, regurgitates textbook information. Complains too much about the lecture slides that he didnt write. Tests are extremely different from practice questions, and are unreasonably wordy. Doesnt show what youve done wrong on previous midterms, no way to see if you were marked fairly. Do not recommend!

He seemed very unenthusiastic about the course and the material in general, and for better or worse is very blunt when replying to students during synchronous lectures. Tests had poorly designed questions, and he seemed all too willing to accept the fact that most students would get sub-50% on tests.

The online delivery of this class was absolutely brutal. The lecture slides are from the textbook manufacturer and he constantly complains about them. Conversely, the final was like a convoluted novel, written with the intent to mislead and curved so that half of the students will fail. Exam is largely theory-based, so don't bother with practice qs

- Boring, unenthusiastic, and uninspired. - Doesn't know how to help. - Fully reliant on the textbook. - Skims through lecture slides and complains about wordiness when the exams are incredibly wordy. - Wouldn't allow us to review our midterm results and see what we got wrong. - Final exam extremely early compared to midterms. - Avoid him.
Self-Study Course, lectures feel more like guidelines.

I really liked Tim.. I know he has really bad ratings but I've sat in his 270 class and took his 201 class. I really liked it. The exams are honestly not that bad and he's really nice. He's really accessible too!

He managed to completely automate his own course: Each week he had pre-recorded 10-minute videos working through a handful of problems, apart from that he was not involved in the course. I essentially had to teach myself all the material, using the textbook he wrote. By far the hardest and worst course I've ever taken. Avoid this professor.
avoid at all costs. final is in less that a week. the grade distribution is still unclear. the TA doesn't know what's going on

I thought I would never say this about a prof but Tim Swartz is an absolute waste of oxygen. Hands down the most lethargic instructor I've ever crossed roads with who has probably taken his career for granted as he makes no effort to help his students. Could not ask him for help during the whole semester and could only contact the TA through email.

He has an unprofessional attitude towards students and lacks compassion. He should never be in a position of authority because he lacks the ability to respectfully engage with students and empathize with their situation. I wish he was more student-oriented and honestly, human-oriented. Students are people too!

Terrible class. I took this class online and the professor was very lazy and did not engage with the class at all. Further, no lectures are added only silly notes that hardly outline the lessons in the chapters. IF YOU TAKE THIS CLASS, YOU WILL BE LEARNING EVERYTHING ON YOUR OWN FROM THE TEXTBOOK. No help from TA or professor.

There are a number of factors that might leave him as the least professional and possibly worst teacher I have encountered at SFU so far. One being that he has been teaching for MANY years and appears to have made zero effort to learn how to use any of the tools provided for teaching.

Tim Swartz for STAT 270? Meh. He seems to be more concerned with getting the lecture material done as fast as he can and never pays attention to those who attend his class and have questions during his lectures. Exams are all from his textbook, 4 midterms, 12 marks each, 1 final, 52 marks (basically every mark you loose is worth 1% of final mark!)

You need to teach yourself everything because there are no online lectures available. The assignments and midterm were straight forward but are only worth 30% of grade. The final, which is worth 70% of your mark, was extremely difficult and had a penalty for wrong answers. Tim is also not very involved in the course; the TA does everything.
Final exam worth 70% and an added penalty for wrong answers had  10% of questions that were specifically omitted from the reading. It's like he designed the course for students to fail.

If you want to take a class where you have no communication with the prof who does not want to be contacted in anyway or care about your success; this is the class for you. Assignments were a breeze and midterm fairly easy. The 70% final is extremely difficult every third answer wrong he takes an additional mark off. Run from him and take in person

I have Stat 101 online but this course was easy and he provided us with the key point via Canvas, so you should just read books and his lectures. Recommend to have this course online with this prof to get basic knowledge about statistics and statistical procedures. By the way our TA was really great!

The midterm is pretty easy if you do all the questions from the assignments. For the final, more than 90% questions are conceptual questions, and the wording can be a bit confusing. But there are fair anyways. Make sure read the textbook thoroughly before you go to the final.

Mr. Swartz is one of the most amazing profs at SFU. Very considerate to students, only a few assignments and all of the reading is done in the textbook which is only 700 pages. He wants students to learn the material well as he makes every chapter fair game for exams. Final is worth 70% and you get penalized for wrong answers so study up! :)

Guys never take STAT270 online from this prof. I took this course online. On campus there is a 3 hour class every week but he will just give you 20 min. online videos a week. You have to do everything on your own. You also have to buy a textbook which is written by the same prof. but I will say don't buy that. Go for other books.

Wow this course is hard, no super hard should I say. I took online course, and material especially book and his video helped me a lot. Final is super hard, do not even think you can just pass the final then pass the course. Like he said, keep practising the questions from the book. You will be fine.
Final exam is worth 70% of the total grade and is completely multiple choice. Wrong answers are penalized. I wouldn't take his class again even though I did fairly well. Avoid if you can
Not a very good professor. What are you doing teaching if you refuse to answer emails about what it's like to be a Statistics major?

Great professor. He covers material very in-depth for the introductory course. He wrote his own textbook (which saves you a lot of money), and you can always ask him for advice. Highly recommend doing the questions that he has in his textbook.

Tim is a very inspirational and helpful prof! He has great enthusiasm  for stats especially in sports. I always enjoyed discussing stats with him, and learned a lot. If you love sports, take courses with him! (but his students, as the TAs, may be harsh on marking the midterm)

I enjoyed class and thought he explained things well. However, I would have liked more examples in the lectures because sometimes there were types of questions on the assignmentsexams that we had never seen before. If you drop by his office he is almost always willing to help if you have questions. Exams are really tough, but grades are curved.

It's an online class so you never see or talk to the prof, theres a TM that manages everything. Theres one midterm - super easy if you do the textbook questions - and one 70% final that was way harder than the midterm. Really took everyone by surprise. Heavy on the last 3 chapters covered.

Took this via distance ed. Everything was through the TA, didn't meet him at all. But like any mathstats class if you read the textbook and do practice questions, you will do well. I thought the exams were fair.
very good
Super super hard final exam like you had learned nothing in the previous 3 months when you see the questions with a super super useless textbook so, avoid him as possible as you could.

This professor is so mean, if you have to talk to him. Normally you wouldn't in a distance ed class, but I had to write to him for an extension due to a specific situation. I provided the note too, and he did not care. I got an "extension" of a couple of hours and he said something really mean in his e-mail back. Avoid if possible!

He is a nice guy, however sometime you will be confused by his questions if you are not a native English speaker. e.g obtain the prob that 2X exceeds Y by more than 4, you want P(2X-Y>4). Exam is super hard but you learn stuff through the process. Have an extra textbook ready and the first thing you want to do is correct the typos in his textbook

While taking this course I did not realize, but this course is really helpful, especially for Macm 201. I pretty much went through the notes, studied the textbook and like the prof said, I did a lot of questions from the back of the chapters. The TA was super helpful as well. I thought this was going to be a hard course but it wasn't as bad.
took his distance class not helpful at all...final worth 55% which covers everything but not that hard(except one or two).I got A
Took his stat270 distance, final is more important than midterm (55% for final grade)which covers everything but not hard to get A
Final is extremely hard. But u can learn lots of things for the further study in the statistics

Just so you know - if you take this course online Swartz will write the midterm and final but have nothing else to do with you. All questions will be answered and grading will be done by a grad student (who doesn't know what questions will be asked on tests). Swartz' book will only confuse you unless you get a decent book (like Devore). Good luck.
Extremely helpful course for future studying in statistic. Exam is hard. But you can learn things in his course.
very good prof but final exam impossible and midterms quit hard

For dist ed: Midterm was tricky, assignments are easy. Final can remove your midterm and be worth 80% of your grade. Final is easy in my opinion, therefore if you study for it you will get a good grade in the course. Definitely not as bad of a course as people make it out to be on here.
im taking his distance education course. no lie, this is a hard course and all, but the questions he gave on the midterm and the assignments were fair.
I like this prof, his midterms and final were quite easy  and I got an A for stat 270.All you need to do is to working out the   questions after each chapter.

I took it through distance. The book is important as well as the assignments. I feel more needs to be given in preparation for the final. the avg was alot lower on the final then the rest of the course.

Course itself is really interesting,Dr Swarts explains material clearly , its just that the course itself is a difficult course. Midterms were fair , although the avg was around 4-6/12 . Although I didnt ended up getting the best grade,however, I found this course to be useful in real life applications. Great proff overal., Useless TAs tho.
i don't know what's going on with distance ED, but i think he is a good prof, he gives hints during lecture about the coming midterms. but he said it's a difficult course, and it is.
Do not take the distance education class. I read the comments, and thought "oh I can handle it" No. You can't. Just save yourself the trouble and frustration. DO NOT TAKE. I MEAN IT. DONT DO IT.

Quite unfair when it comes to marking and scaling. Took a distant education course with him and regretted it. Not enough solved problems given. Exams are pretty difficult and he does not scale very much.

How can a prof like him still teaching in SFU? I don't think students would like to study stats anymore after taking his class. Exams are super difficult (avg 30-40%)~ and he treats Dis Ed students like nothing (zero help) His text book exercises are quite useless because there no answers, so you don't know you do them right or not.
I took BUEC232 before taking this course, but it still looks like OMGosh for me....If you read his textbook, you will find he made easy things complicated.......
If not the worst prof, definitely the top 5. Such a hypocrite with a cheesy sense of humour. The most useless prof I've ever encountered.

I took it as distance education. I had never seen him in my whole semester and his TA is the only one that you will communicate with. TA was doing an awesome job for answering students' questions.  The mt was not so hard and the final was 60 m/c. He curved the whole class pretty fair. If u read the book regularly u will be fine.
He is such a bad teacher. I took his distance ED class. For the final you get penalized (50 multiple choice): correct-incorrect/3. Horrible experience
Wow! Just took the final and got destroyed. I really hope he keeps his word though. He mentioned that unless you get 30%, he wouldn't fail you. I'm praying!

He's very clear and slow at explaining things, and patient while answering questions. Quizzes are fair if you catch up with the lectures(lots of hints on quizzes!) and study the sample quizzes. Final is a bit harder but the marking scheme is not that terrible. Overall he's generous on the final grade. I got 67% and ended with an A.

This is a difficult course only because there are loads of material to cover. Pay attention and fully understand the problems covered in class and you will do well, since similar problems often appear on the midterms and exam.

I came into this class expecting a hardass, but he wasn't like any one of those comments described him to be. He's very approachable, funny, and obviously knows his stuff. Questions on mt's are really easy, some are right from his notes. Go to his class, because the stuff he talks about are quite useful on the mt's hint hint...

Easy A+! Took it distance ed, so never got to see him - TA did everything. I guess he only designs the exams...but compared to the comments below, questions were super easy. Course content is easy enough to teach yourself, and grades are scaled pretty well. Great preparation course for BUEC 232/STAT 270. 2-sided cheatsheet allowed too =)

This is one of the worse profs ever...quizzes and the final are impossible to do unless you REALLY FULLY understand material and are able to comprehend any kind of question he can throw at you in any kind of wording.  If you ask him for help, he explains it the same way and expects you to understand it the 2nd time.  HORRIBLE.....
Hard but fun course. Useful material for real life - but meant for people with a math brain. If taking this for entrance req for business, take BUEC instead if you don't like mathematical concepts.
HOLY ****! I attended every lecture. Went to the workshop every week, I TRIED on this course. Grading scheme is tough! You either get it right or it's wrong. No partial marks = fail

Very hard course, especially with him its even harder. His midterms are fairly hard but still rather ok. His final is just out of this world! totally unexpected, and unless you REALLY know your stuff, you won't do well. Avoid taking his stat!

Very hard course.  Class average for every quiz was below 50%.  His notes are really hard to read because he just writes them out by hand puts scanned copies onto the course website.  I would not take a course with this instructor again.

how the hell are you above the class average before the final and end up with a C-. Idiot deceived us by telling us he was gonna scale it better relative to past terms. Quizzes are too hard and tricky. For the type of course that is it (bs) I put in enough effort and was thorougly screwed!
SIGH  JUST PASS    His queizs are too hard
hard

If you think you're failing, keep your head up.  Try as hard as you can, and you'll probably be surprised at your final grade. For cheat sheet, WRITE DOWN AS MANY PROBLEMS ON IT AS YOU CAN!!! Final will use problems you didn't think were important, but in a different way. Helped me tons!  I had below 50% going into final and came out with a B.

His tests are brutal, his notes suck. He writes with an overhead marker on paper and then photocopies it for us to download.  Barely legible when you print it.  His tests are way too hard because the questions are vague. the class average on every test is well below 50%.  I dont understand why they have to be so difficult!
No Comments

I flunked this course. I took his STAT270 long time ago, and I found it not bad. But when I took STAT350 it's a whole different world. He was mean. He thought the students are as smart as him. His exams are so hard that it's useless reading and memorizing his notes. DON'T take any of his courses.

His lecture was well organized & logical  The quizzes are tricky but u can do them well by practising on old quizzes which are on his web site. However, he is not so care about his students. His Off.H was very little and he is not so patient w/ students who have Qs.

His quizzes and final exam can demonstrate how well you learn this course. Although his quizzes are tricky,you can overcome it throught practising the past quizzes posted on line. Also, he will give you a fair grade if you study well at the end.

His teaching is fine with helpful notes and clear clarifications in class. Quizzes are easier if you actually spend your time going to class and do practice quizzes which are always available online. Listen to his hints in class, and spend some time doing practice problems.
Shitwartz, it's not ur fault. Just that the whole school is kidding us.

The worst stat prof. he doesn't really care about his students. As he said in class, his "main interest is doing research, not teaching." In stat 350, more than a half of the class got c or less. The median grade was C-.

I have no clue how I managed to pass this course.
 All I know is giving students killer hard material in an "intro" stats course doesn't help when you take higher-level courses.  You spend too much time worrying rather than learning!
He expects alot from students. He thinks everyone should be able to do the questions he designs.  He expects every students to be as smart as he is.
stat was easy before, but not anymore with swartz. His quizes are hard, final is hard with all the proofs you need to do. Swartz is A in teaching ability though.
the quizes are very tough and the final is easy. ignore the quizes if u did bad. he will just count 100% for the final.
bad guy

Run away if he's teaching~!! His quizes are tricky; they are design for two types of student: supersmart and genius; so, if you r super smart, you can past; but u can only get good mark if u r a genius.  Too many material in this class & he teach 2 fast!!
Although his lectures are through and have structure, they are too easy and his exams are impossible. Don't take his courses unless you plan to fail.

He prints out the notes then comes to class and reads them.. His lectures are bland and uninformative. there are no reccomended problems to study, so you're on your own to study. you're better off reading the text than going to class.
final is just way too hard!!!
No Comments
He's kind of bland, but goes over the material quite well, and is a nice guy.
he is a genius, ask him on biddng
No Comments
his final is really hard. his quizzes are relatively easy.
beware of Tim Shady's marking SCHEMES
That's no point of studying his test, it's mission impossible !
He is very precise and clear, but the midterms and exams are hell!!!
